title: News
text:
I will join Pepperdine University as a tenure-track Assistant Professor of Data Science beginning Fall 2026.
My manuscript, Financial NeQTL: A Genomics-Inspired Bayesian Approach to Market Structure Inference, was completed and is currently under review.
I contributed to the Drexel University team’s FY 2026 Supporting Effective Educator Development grant proposal, providing input on statistics, AI integration, evaluation design, and performance measures.

title: News
text:
I will join Pepperdine University as a tenure-track Assistant Professor of Data Science beginning Fall 2026.
My manuscript, Financial NeQTL: A Genomics-Inspired Bayesian Approach to Market Structure Inference, was completed and is currently under review.
I contributed to the Drexel University team’s FY 2026 Supporting Effective Educator Development grant proposal, providing input on statistics, AI integration, evaluation design, and performance measures.

title: News
text:
2025

My paper on literature-derived duplicate records in the FDA Adverse Event Reporting System database was selected as Editor’s Choice for the February issue of Canadian Journal of Physiology and Pharmacology.
I received the Tharp Endowed Award from the College of Arts and Sciences for excellence in teaching and research.
I continued research on Financial NeQTL / FiNeQTL models for financial market prediction.

title: News
text:
2024

I began my postdoctoral appointment at the University of South Florida on August 7, 2024.
I completed my Ph.D. in Mathematics with a concentration in Statistics at the University of South Florida on May 2nd, 2024.
I presented research on deception detection, sentiment analysis, and transformer-based statistical learning at academic conferences.

title: News
text:
2022

Passed the Specialty Qualifying Exam at the Ph.D. level in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at the University of South Florida on March 22, 2022.

title: News
text:
2021

Passed the Qualifying Exam on Mathematical Statistics at the Ph.D. level in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at the University of South Florida on August 16, 2021.
Passed the Qualifying Exam on Applied Statistics at the Ph.D. level in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at the University of South Florida on May 15, 2021.

title: News
text:
2020

Admitted to the Ph.D. program in Mathematics with a concentration in Statistics at the University of South Florida beginning Fall 2020.
Successfully defended my master’s thesis, Bayesian Reliability Analysis for Optical Media Using Accelerated Degradation Test Data, at the University of South Florida.

title: Research
text: My research focuses on developing statistical and data science methods for complex, high-dimensional, and interdisciplinary problems.

title: Research
text:
Research Areas

Bayesian Modeling and Structured Inference
I am interested in Bayesian methods, structured dependence modeling, and interpretable statistical learning.


Financial NeQTL / FiNeQTL
My current research develops genomics-inspired Bayesian frameworks for financial market prediction. This work adapts ideas from eQTL neighborhood structure to model relationships among sectors, industries, stock prices, and trading volume.


Human-Centered AI
I am interested in responsible and human-centered AI systems that support decision-making, implementation, teaching, and research.


Pharmacovigilance and Healthcare Data Science
My prior work includes medication safety, adverse event detection, drug-drug interaction analysis, and pharmacovigilance studies using large-scale healthcare databases.


Statistics and Data Science Education
I also work on improving statistics and data science education through applied examples, computational tools, and accessible teaching materials.

title: Kun (Amanda) Bu, Ph.D.
text:
I am an incoming tenure-track Assistant Professor of Data Science at Pepperdine University, beginning Fall 2026. I am currently a postdoctoral scholar at the University of South Florida.
My research lies at the intersection of statistics, data science, Bayesian modeling, and human-centered AI, with applications in financial market prediction, pharmacovigilance, healthcare data science, and statistics education.

title: Kun (Amanda) Bu, Ph.D.
text:
I am an incoming tenure-track Assistant Professor of Data Science at Pepperdine University, beginning Fall 2026. I am currently a postdoctoral scholar at the University of South Florida.
My research lies at the intersection of statistics, data science, Bayesian modeling, and human-centered AI, with applications in financial market prediction, pharmacovigilance, healthcare data science, and statistics education.

title: Kun (Amanda) Bu, Ph.D.
text:
Research Interests

Bayesian modeling and structured statistical inference
Financial NeQTL / FiNeQTL models for interpretable market prediction
Human-centered AI and AI-supported decision-making
Pharmacovigilance, medication safety, and healthcare data science
Statistics and data science education

title: Kun (Amanda) Bu, Ph.D.
text:
Recent Updates

I will join Pepperdine University as a tenure-track Assistant Professor of Data Science in Fall 2026.
I am developing Financial NeQTL / FiNeQTL models for interpretable financial market prediction.
I continue interdisciplinary research in statistics, healthcare, finance, AI, and education.
